title: UX Spec — Experiencia Ideal (lead capture + SEO) · Sports World
body:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Version | v1.0 (derived from and corrected against 01_UX_Specification_v4_2_10.docx) |
| Date | 2026-06-10 |
| Authors | Product · Design · Engineering · QA (co-authorship pending sign-off) |
| Status | In review |
| Output stack | React + Tailwind `[ASSUMPTION — the demo uses inline styles; confirm tokens in Tailwind]` |
| Handoff tool | `[TO BE DEFINED — link Figma inspect / Zeplin]` |
| Single source of truth (data/rules) | sw_experiencia_ideal_demo_v6_FINAL.jsx + this document |

> **Method note.** This is a **content-complete** document: it combines the strategic/UX layer of the `ux-spec` standard (§1–§10) with the **1:1 transfer** of the ENTIRE original technical document (Parts 1–6 + Appendices A–H: 144 sections and 24 tables, see «Technical Part»). Nothing from the original is left out; the strategic layer adds rationale, personas, journey, tokens, POUR accessibility, conversion, acceptance criteria and metrics.

---

title: 1.1 Reasoning chain (Why → Who → What → How)
body:
- **Why (SMART goal).** Double the site's organic traffic: go from **80,000 to 160,000 monthly visits in 3 months**, making the site much more findable on Google through an SEO strategy applied to a **new hub and pagination structure**. Example lever: a **"lose weight" hub** captures one of the highest-volume searches in Mexico and, on its own, has the potential to double traffic.
  - **Secondary goal:** double (**2x**) the number of **qualified leads** that reach the advisor.
  - **Tertiary goal:** reduce the **customer response time** through the **voice agent** (immediate 24/7 attention).
- **Who (actors).**
  - *Primary:* a person in Mexico searching on Google ("how to lose weight", "gym near me", "gain muscle mass") who lands on a hub.
  - *Secondary:* the **Advisor** (sales) who receives the brief and schedules the guided visit; the **voice agent** that responds and coordinates.
  - *Off-stage:* trainers who define exercises in the first session; the marketing/SEO team that governs the hubs.
- **What (measurable behavior / Jobs to be Done).** The visitor must: **find** the site on Google → **complete** the Experiencia Ideal questionnaire → **leave** their contact details → **schedule** the guided visit. Measured by: organic traffic, questionnaire completion rate, qualified leads and time to first response.
- **How (tactics/UI).** An **SEO hub** architecture (indexable, high-volume pages) that feeds the **Experiencia Ideal** flow: a guided 19-question questionnaire that delivers a personalized recommendation (Block 1 weights · Block 2 cardio · Block 3 classes) + contact capture + brief for the advisor + voice agent.

title: 1.2 Macro justification (business strategy)
body: The growth engine is **structural SEO**, not paid advertising. Today the site receives 80,000 visits/month; the ceiling is limited by the **information architecture**: few indexable pages pointing to high-volume searches. The new structure creates **thematic hubs** (lose weight, muscle mass, cardiovascular health, etc.) and **paginated pages** of clubs/classes, multiplying the indexable surface and relevance. Each hub is at once an **SEO entry point** and the start of the **conversion funnel** (Experiencia Ideal). Thus, the same structural change serves all three goals: more traffic, more qualified leads and faster response.

title: 1.3 Micro justification (specific decisions)
body:
| Decision | Why this one and not another |
|---|---|
| **Single guided questionnaire** (19 questions) instead of a short form | It is an **interactive tool of value** (an "ideal experience" calculator): the user provides data in exchange for a personalized recommendation, which mitigates the bounce of long forms. *Risk:* it is still long → drop-off per question is measured (see §10) and progressive profiling is evaluated if drop-off exceeds the threshold. |
| **Brand red `#E6282A`** reserved for CTA and accents | It signals action/conversion; it is never used in text blocks so as not to dilute the hierarchy. |
| **Three color blocks** (blue/green/gray) for the recommendation | They cognitively segment the three training components; they reduce load by separating "what I do with weights / cardio / classes". |
| **Contact capture AFTER the result** | The user has already received value (their recommendation); asking for data at that moment maximizes conversion and lead quality. |
| **Accessible language, no jargon** ("muscle growth", not "hypertrophy") | The target audience is not expert; jargon alienates and reduces conversion (see `ux-writing`). |
| **Goal-oriented subgroup names** (6) instead of ACSM nomenclature | The user recognizes themselves in their goal ("Lose weight"), not in a technical physiology term. |

---

title: 2.1 Personas
body:
**P1 — Daniela, 34, looking to lose weight (primary).**
She searches Google from her phone for "how to lose weight fast" or "gym near home". Context: little time, somewhat intimidated by the gym. Goal: to look and feel better. Frustration: she doesn't know where to start or whether a gym is for her. **Conversion trigger:** a clear, non-judgmental recommendation + a guided visit.

**P2 — Marco, 27, wants to gain muscle mass (primary).**
Intermediate, coming from another gym. He searches for "muscle mass routine" / "best gym". He wants measurable progress and equipment. Frustration: generic plans. **Trigger:** seeing that the recommendation adapts to his level.

**P3 — Sales advisor (secondary, internal).**
Receives the **brief** with the lead's profile and their flags (beginner, pregnancy, injury, GLP-1, family with children). Goal: convert the visit into a membership without re-asking what has already been answered. Frustration: cold or context-less leads.

**P4 — Voice agent (secondary, system).**
Attends and coordinates 24/7. Goal: respond instantly and schedule; transfer to a human when it doesn't understand.

title: 2.2 Customer Journey (from Google to membership)
body:
```mermaid
journey
  title Experiencia Ideal — from search to visit
  section Discover (SEO)
    Searches Google "lose weight": 3: Daniela
    Enters the Lose Weight hub: 4: Daniela
  section Evaluate
    Reads the hub and understands the proposition: 4: Daniela
    Starts "Experiencia Ideal": 4: Daniela
  section Qualify
    Answers the questionnaire (Q1-Q19): 3: Daniela
    Receives her personalized recommendation: 5: Daniela
  section Convert
    Leaves her contact details: 4: Daniela
    Schedules the guided visit: 5: Daniela
  section Close (human)
    Advisor receives the brief and attends to her: 5: Advisor
    Voice agent confirms the appointment: 5: Agent
```

---

title: 3. Flows and Transition Diagram
body:
All branches (not just the happy path). System phases: `welcome · questionnaire · loading · result · contact_capture · schedule · briefing · error`. ```mermaid flowchart TD G[Google / SEO] --> H[Thematic hub
e.g. Lose Weight] H --> W[welcome] W --> Q[questionnaire Q1-Q19] Q -->|Q2=Woman| Q12b[Q12b pregnancy/postpartum] Q -->|Q14 with children| Q14b[Q14b children <12] Q -->|drop-off| EXIT([Exits — drop-off is measured]) Q --> L[loading
1 LLM call] L -->|JSON ok| R[result
Experiencia Ideal] L -->|malformed JSON| RF[result with safe fallback] R --> CC[contact_capture
last name + phone + email] CC -->|Back| R CC -->|3 valid fields| S[schedule] S -->|Back| CC S --> B[briefing
advisor brief] R -.network error.-> ERR[error + retry] L -.timeout.-> ERR ``` **Safety filter (YMYL):** before building Block 3 (classes), the engine applies the **hard contraindication filter** (5 conditions: injury, cardiovascular, pregnancy, postpartum, bariatric). Contraindicated classes never appear. Full detail in the technical document (Rule 14b). ---

title: 4.1 Thematic SEO hub (e.g. `/perder-peso`)
body:
- **Purpose:** capture high-intent organic traffic and route it to Experiencia Ideal.
- **Layout and dimensions:** 12-column grid; max container 1200px; padding 16px mobile / 24px desktop; breakpoints 360 / 768 / 1024 / 1440px.
- **SEO content (minimum per hub):** H1 with the main keyword; 600–900 words of useful text; FAQ with `schema.org/FAQPage`; internal links to related clubs and classes; CTA "Descubre tu experiencia ideal".
- **Metadata:** `<title>` ≤ 60 chars, `meta description` ≤ 155 chars, canonical, Open Graph; `lang="es-MX"`.
- **Pagination:** club/class listings with logical `rel=next/prev` and clean URLs `/clubes/cdmx/pagina-2`; avoids duplicate content with canonical.
- **Main CTA:** red button `#E6282A` → starts `welcome`.
- **Non-functional requirement:** **LCP < 2.5 s**, **CLS < 0.1**, **INP < 200 ms** (Core Web Vitals — they affect SEO ranking).

title: 4.2 Questionnaire (`questionnaire`, Q1–Q19)
body:
- **Purpose:** qualify and personalize; collect the lead's data.
- **Structure:** 16 base questions (Q1–Q16) + conditionals **Q11** (pause), **Q12b** (pregnancy/postpartum, if Q2=Woman), **Q14b** (children <12) + optional weight questions **Q17–Q19**.
- **One step per screen**, progress bar, "Continuar" button disabled until answered.
- **Interactive states:** option `default / hover / focus-visible / selected / disabled`; button `default / hover / active / disabled / loading`.
- **Inline validation (real-time):**
  - Q1 Name: required, ≥ 2 characters.
  - Q8 days / Q7 schedules: multi-select, ≥ 1.
  - Q16 ZIP code **or** zone (XOR): ZIP = 5 numeric digits.
- **Content (UX writing):** questions in Mexican Spanish, active voice, no jargon. Gender agreement if Q2=Woman (Q3, Q13, Q14).
- **Non-functional requirement:** transition between questions < 100 ms; state persisted on the client so answers are not lost on reload.

title: 4.3 Result — Experiencia Ideal (`result`)
body:
- **Purpose:** deliver the personalized recommendation (the "value" in exchange for the data).
- **Visual architecture:** red top bar 4px `#E6282A`; 4 summary cards (goal, level, schedule, who they train with); pink CTA banner `#FFF4F4`/border `#F3B9BC`; 3 block cards (01 blue `#EEF5FF` · 02 green `#EDF8F1` · 03 gray `#F3F4F6`); amber safety section `#FFF6E7` with contextual copy; fixed legal note.
- **Block 1 (weights):** one of **6 accessible names** according to goal Q4; never lists equipment ("Tu entrenador define los ejercicios y el peso en la primera sesión").
- **Block 2 (cardio):** machine + duration + pace in plain language ("ritmo conversacional", not "Zone 2").
- **Block 3 (classes):** top 2 recommended classes after the contraindication filter, or Personal Training as an alternative.
- **Non-functional requirement:** render with fallback data if the LLM returns invalid JSON (graceful degradation, no blank screen).

title: 4.4 Contact capture (`contact_capture`)
body:
- **Purpose:** convert interest into a contactable lead. It appears **between** `result` and `schedule`; you cannot schedule without completing it.
- **Header:** "Antes de agendar" · "{Nombre}, necesitamos un par de datos para confirmar tu visita."
- **Fields, validation and errors (verbatim):**

| Field | Validation | Inline error |
|---|---|---|
| Apellido | `trim().length ≥ 2` | "Ingresa tu apellido (mínimo 2 letras)" |
| Celular | exactly **10 digits** | "Ingresa un número de 10 dígitos" |
| Correo | `/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/` | "Ingresa un correo electrónico válido" |

- **Privacy:** "Tus datos se usan únicamente para coordinar tu visita guiada. No los compartimos con terceros."
- **"Continuar" button states:** red when the 3 fields are valid; gray disabled otherwise.
- **Real-time validation:** the email error appears **while typing**, not on submit.

title: 4.5 Schedule and Brief (`schedule`, `briefing`)
body:
- `schedule`: day/time selection; "Volver" returns to `contact_capture`.
- `briefing`: advisor brief (10 sections, 5 generated by the LLM) with safety flags. Detail in the technical document (Appendix G).

---

title: 5. Edge Cases and Conditional States Matrix
body:
| Condition | Trigger | UI behavior | Message |
|---|---|---|---|
| Empty state (no valid classes) | All classes contraindicated | Block 3 shows Personal Training as an alternative | "Tu Advisor define el detalle en la visita." |
| Server error / LLM timeout | 5xx or high latency | Render with safe fallback; retry option | "No pudimos generar tu experiencia. Reintentar." |
| Malformed JSON from the LLM | Parse fails | Page renders hardcoded sections; empty arrays | — (silent) |
| Extremely long text | Very long name/club | Wrap + `text-overflow: ellipsis` on chips | — |
| Slow connection | High latency | Skeleton in `loading` + spinner; no blocking | — |
| No club coverage nearby | ZIP/zone with no club | Shows other clubs + TooFar note | "El club más cercano está a {distancia}." |
| Pregnancy / postpartum / injury / bariatric | Q12/Q12b/Q17 | Hard class filter + safety message | Contextual safety copy (§4.3) |
| FitKidz with no class names (10 clubs) | State B | Generic red section, no chips | "Tu Advisor te compartirá las actividades para tus hijos." |
| Questionnaire drop-off | Closes before Q19 | The last question viewed is recorded | — (analytics event) |

> Reviewed with QA in the design phase: **`[TO BE DEFINED — schedule review with QA]`**.

---

title: 6. Design System and Tokens
body:
- **Style guide:** see `DESIGN.md` (tokens + rules for AI agents).
- **Tokens (DTCG/JSON):** palette extracted from the demo (not invented):

| Role | Token | Value |
|---|---|---|
| Action / brand | `color.brand.primary` | `#E6282A` |
| Ink (text) | `color.text.ink` | `#1D1D1B` |
| Secondary text | `color.text.muted` | `#6B6B68` |
| Disabled text | `color.text.disabled` | `#A8A8A6` |
| Border | `color.border.default` | `#E5E5E3` |
| Surface | `color.surface.base` | `#F5F5F4` |
| Block 01 (weights) | `color.block.strength` | `#EEF5FF` |
| Block 02 (cardio) | `color.block.cardio` | `#EDF8F1` |
| Block 03 (classes) | `color.block.classes` | `#F3F4F6` |
| CTA banner | `color.cta.bannerBg` / `color.cta.bannerBorder` | `#FFF4F4` / `#F3B9BC` |
| Safety (YMYL) | `color.safety.bg` | `#FFF6E7` |

- **Reused components/patterns:** block card, summary card, chip/pill, CTA banner, safety section, field with inline validation, progress bar.

---

title: 7. Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA / EAA) — POUR
body: > Legal obligation (EAA, June 2025), not optional. Preventive mapping.

title: Perceivable
body:
- **Contrast:** validate each text token over its background ≥ **4.5:1** (normal text) / **3:1** (large). Known risk: red `#E6282A` on white gives ~4.0:1 → **do not use it for small text**, only for button backgrounds with white text or for large iconography. `[ACTION: validate with a contrast linter]`
- **Not color alone:** the "selected" state of an option uses **border + check**, not just color. The safety section uses an **"!" icon + text**, not just the amber.
- **Alt text:** every hub/club image carries a descriptive `alt` (syntax: "{type} at {club}, {action}").

title: Operable
body:
- Logical **tab order**: progress → options → Continue. Visible focus (`focus-visible` 2px ring).
- No control depends on gesture alone; multi-select operable by keyboard (Space/Enter).

title: Understandable
body:
- `lang="es-MX"` declared. Error messages in active voice and specific ("Ingresa un número de 10 dígitos").
- Consistent gender agreement (Q2=Woman).

title: Robust
body:
- Dynamic changes announced with `aria-live="polite"` (appointment confirmation, validation errors). `role="alert"` on submission errors.
- Semantic markup: `<fieldset>/<legend>` per question, `<label>` per field.

**Per-screen checklist:** contrast ✔ · alt ✔ · tab order ✔ · visible focus ✔ · language ✔ · aria-live ✔ → run an audit with a plugin before front-end.

---

title: 8. Handoff and Synchronization
body:
- **Source of truth:** demo `sw_experiencia_ideal_demo_v6_FINAL.jsx` (behavior) + this spec (rationale) + `DESIGN.md` (tokens). Visual handoff: `[TO BE DEFINED — Figma inspect]`.
- **Assets:** vector icons (SVG), exportable; "SPORTS WORLD" logo (weight 800).
- **Design Drift risk mitigated by:** centralized tokens (`DESIGN.md`) and code-backed components (the demo is the functional reference).

---

title: 9. Acceptance Criteria
body:
- [ ] Each hub renders an H1 with keyword, FAQ with structured data, canonical and valid metadata.
- [ ] Core Web Vitals in the green on mobile (LCP < 2.5 s, CLS < 0.1, INP < 200 ms).
- [ ] The questionnaire advances one question per screen; Q11/Q12b/Q14b appear only with their condition.
- [ ] You cannot reach `schedule` without the 3 valid contact details.
- [ ] Block 3 never shows a contraindicated class according to Q12/Q12b/Q17.
- [ ] If the LLM fails, the result page renders with a fallback (no blank screen).
- [ ] All error texts are inline, in active voice and specific.
- [ ] Contrast of all text/background pairs ≥ 4.5:1 (or 3:1 large), validated by a linter.
- [ ] Dynamic changes announced to screen readers (aria-live).
- [ ] `lang="es-MX"` declared on all pages.

---

title: 10.1 KPIs
body:
| Metric | Starting point | Goal (3 months) | Type |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Monthly organic traffic** | **80,000 visits** | **160,000 (2x)** | Primary KPI |
| **Qualified leads / month** | `[ASSUMPTION: 1,000]` | **2x** | Secondary |
| **Time to first response** (voice agent) | `[ASSUMPTION: hours]` | **< 1 min, 24/7** | Secondary |
| Questionnaire completion rate | `[ASSUMPTION: 40%]` | `[ASSUMPTION: ≥ 55%]` | Diagnostic |
| Scheduling rate (guided visit) | `[ASSUMPTION: 12%]` | `[ASSUMPTION: ≥ 20%]` | Conversion |

> Figures marked `[ASSUMPTION]` are for reference; replace with real analytics data.
